# The Human Origin

> Six film disciplines applied to the patterns that run organisations. A diagnostic and re-architecture of the survival patterns running leadership rooms — applied in real meetings and real decisions. Founded by Tom Waldek, former 25-year film professional (writing, directing, acting, editing, producing, intimacy coordination). Based in Europe, working in English and German across the DACH region and the EU.

## Commitments (trust signals)
- The First Conversation: 45 minutes. No pitch. No fee. No follow-up unless you ask.
- Confidentiality: Confidential by default. What surfaces in this work stays with you. No logos, no case studies, no names — ever.
- The Fit Test: If the work isn't the right fit for your organisation, I will tell you first.

## Where This Operates (positioning statement)
Most leadership work addresses what people consciously reflect on, or what they're taught. The patterns this work surfaces run beneath that layer — in how the leader actually moves before words, in real meetings, in the decisions already in motion before anyone chooses them. That's the room this work enters.

## Canonical pages

- [Homepage (English)](https://thehumanorigin.org/en/): The full methodology, positioning, offer, and interactive Pulse Check diagnostic.
- [Homepage (German)](https://thehumanorigin.org/de/): Gesamte Methodik, Positionierung, Angebot und interaktiver Pulscheck.
- [Language selector](https://thehumanorigin.org/): Root page with English/German language choice.

## The methodology

The Human Origin applies six film-craft disciplines to the patterns that run leadership rooms. Unlike leadership development (which adds skills to the person), this work removes the patterns running them. The methodology is grounded in 25 years of film-set observation and resonates with peer-reviewed research: Psychological Safety (Amy Edmondson), Emotional Contagion (Sigal Barsade), the Power Paradox (Dacher Keltner), Logotherapy (Viktor Frankl), and Polyvagal Theory (Stephen Porges).

## The Architecture — how the methodology connects

The Human Origin methodology is not six disciplines applied independently. It is six film-craft disciplines applied to nine diagnostic zones of organisational pattern, each zone in resonance with peer-reviewed research, each discipline operating in a specific engagement phase. The mapping:

| Discipline | Frameworks Resonated With | Diagnostic Zone | What Changes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 01 Writing | Frankl · Barsade | Meaning Access · Narrative Gap | The story told matches the story lived |
| 02 Directing | Edmondson · Porges | Survival Architecture · Safety & Honesty | Conditions where truth shows up, not silence |
| 03 Acting | Barsade · Keltner | Peer Dynamics · The Origin | Real presence replaces performed safety |
| 04 Editing | Keltner · Edmondson | External Trust Signal | Cutting what keeps people hiding |
| 05 Producing | Porges | Relational Pattern | The backbone so people stop holding it alone |
| 06 Intimacy Coordination | Brown · Rodis (SAG-AFTRA) | Attachment · Safety & Honesty | Vulnerability without exposure |

When the Trust Architecture Diagnostic runs, this is the map being populated. When Origin Work begins, this is the architecture that shifts.

## Proprietary vocabulary (The Human Origin Lexicon)

- **Survival Architecture** — The hidden operating system an organisation defaults to under pressure and rarely switches off. A state in which how the leader actually moves — under the title, under the role — silently dictates the conditions of safety, dissent, and decision-making for the entire team. Not a metaphor — readable in any film-set room.
- **Trust Architecture** — The underlying structural conditions that determine whether trust can form in an organisation. The architecture underneath performance, grounded in how the leader actually moves.
- **Return to Origin** — The movement by which a leader and their organisation move from survival architecture back to access of capacity: creative intelligence, emotional depth, meaning, and decisions made from the person rather than the pattern.
- **Origin Work** — The 3–5 month engagement phase. Dissolves survival patterns where they actually operate — in live conversations, real decisions, the moments people normally perform through.
- **The Trust Cascade** — The sequence by which how the leader actually moves propagates outward from the leader to the leadership table to the team to clients and partners.
- **Six Film Disciplines** — Writing, Directing, Acting, Editing, Producing, Intimacy Coordination. The diagnostic system of The Human Origin methodology.

## Key statements (quotable, attributed to Tom Waldek)

- "The organisation doesn't copy the leader's strategy. It copies how the leader actually moves."
- "Most organisations perform the company they wish they were — not the one they actually are."
- "Truth leads. Facade costs."
- "Control replaces connection. Performance replaces presence."
- "Performance fills the void where purpose used to live."
- "25 years in film taught me to read rooms, people, emotions — and to tell what's authentic from what's performed."

## Offer (transparent)

- **Entry Conversation** — 45 minutes, no fee. One question, no pitch.
- **Diagnostic Debrief** (optional, standalone) — 90 minutes, €2,500 fixed fee.
- **Trust Architecture Diagnostic** — 3–4 weeks, €12,000. Five observation contexts, one-on-one conversations, a map of where survival patterns cost the most.
- **Origin Work** and **Retained Advisory** — by application after the diagnostic. Fee scales with scope.

## Target audience

Scale-up founders at Series B–D; CHROs at mid-market 500–3,000 employee organisations; leaders in transition (new CEOs at 90 days, post-merger integration, post-restructure). European and DACH market, English and German.

## Contact

tom@thehumanorigin.org

## About the founder

Tom Waldek spent 25 years in professional film across Europe — writing, directing, acting, editing, producing, intimacy coordination. He brings those six disciplines to the patterns that run leadership rooms, as founder of The Human Origin.

## Scientific grounding (frameworks this work resonates with)

- Edmondson, A.C. (2018). The Fearless Organization.
- Barsade, S.G. (2002). The Ripple Effect: Emotional Contagion in Groups.
- Keltner, D. (2016). The Power Paradox.
- Frankl, V.E. (1946). Man's Search for Meaning.
- Brown, B. (2012). Daring Greatly.
- Porges, S.W. (2011). The Polyvagal Theory.
